name: Easy Sweet and Sour Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb Lean pork butt or fresh ham
recipeInstructions:
PART 1:
1. Cut pork into 1/2 x 1/2 x 1 inch rectangles. Marinate with a mixture of salt, soya sauce, and sherry for at least half an hour. While the meat is marinating, prepare Part 2.
2. Add 2 tablespoons oil to a wok and heat until smoking. Use a slotted spoon to lift pork pieces from the marinade and place them in the wok. Brown the meat on all sides. Lower the heat slightly to medium high and continue cooking for a full 15 minutes, stirring occasionally with a turner.
3. Place the meat in a serving dish. Pour the sauce from Part 2 over it.
PART 2:
1. In a small 1-quart pot, combine white sugar and vinegar. Bring to a boil until the sugar is dissolved. Stir in corn starch mixed with 1/4 cup water. Add this mixture to the vinegar-sugar boil. Lower the heat to medium low, then add pineapple juice and tomato soup. Simmer for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce turns from milky red to a clear reddish yellow.
2. Add optional embellishments such as pineapple chunks, shredded raw carrot, half a green pepper (shredded), tomato wedges, and mixed sweet pickles. Cook for an additional 2 minutes before serving over Part 1.
Note: Pork spare ribs cut into 1 1/2 inches lengths may be used instead of pork butt. However, spare ribs contain more fat than fresh ham or pork butt, so use 1 tablespoon less oil in stir-frying the ribs or discard the accumulated fat in the wok when the ribs are finished cooking. Part 2 (without embellishments) can be made in larger quantities and stored in a covered plastic container in the refrigerator for up to 3 weeks.
